steals.steal: AI citation gap as buying trigger
steals.detail: 'Why does my CEO see our competitors in ChatGPT and not us?' — frames visibility as a conversion leak before the landing page. Steal: added to hero as '94% of B2B buyers ask AI before they buy. Your competitors show up in ChatGPT. You don't.'

steals.steal: 'Your brand is either in the answer or it isn't' positioning
steals.detail: Binary framing of AI visibility. No middle ground. Nebula should use: 'If your landing page doesn't convert, AI doesn't recommend it. If AI doesn't recommend you, buyers never see you.'
Citation frequency (0-25), recency (0-25), sentiment (0-25), share of voice vs competitors (0-25). Adaptable as new audit dimension — 'AI Citation Score' alongside headline/CTA/trust/offer/speed.

Directly states limits of the tool before buyer has to discover them. 'This won't rewrite your site, fix your targeting, or run A/B tests. It tells you what to fix and why.' Steal: added to index.html before testimonials as 'What the audit won't do for you' grid.
Each LLM trains on different sources — ChatGPT weights Reuters/FT, Claude weights Forbes, Perplexity weights TIME/Axios. Nebula should build a reference table for AI citation fix delivery.

Asks the AI engine the exact 5 queries a buyer types: 'best X for Y', 'X vs Z', 'how to [JTB]', 'X for [vertical]', 'Z alternatives'. Nebula should integrate this query set into the audit pipeline for the AI visibility dimension.

Free Claude project does the audit; paid service does the placements. Same shape as Nebula's free audit → $97 Fix Pack. SwayyEm charges $5k/mo minimum — validates Nebula's $97 price as extreme low-end and suggests room to raise.

steals.steal: Scored to 0-100 with specific action thresholds
steals.detail: Not vague — 'your visibility is 42/100. fix these 3 things.' Mirrors Nebula's 5-dimension scoring. Validation of the audit-score-into-action format.

Matt's Claude system prompt includes: 'You explicitly call out what the user can't do without editorial relationships.' Nebula should adopt this in our audit output: 'Fix priority 1 you can do in 30 min. Fix priority 3 requires a developer. Fix priority 5 requires a PR agency.'

Designed to be handed to CMO in 1 page — visibility score, top 3 gaps, 5 publication targets, 30-day plan. Nebula audit output should be structured the same way: score, top 3 leaks, dollar estimate, 3-day plan.

nebula_advantage: Self-serve checkout at $97 vs SwayyEm's $5k/mo minimum
Automated instant audit vs manual Claude project setup
Combines landing page + AI visibility in one scan
Trigger-targeted outbound (ad bleed) drives ICP to audit
swayyem_advantage: Honesty boundaries coded into system prompt

action_items.action: Add AI citation score as 6th audit dimension
Build LLM publication weightings reference table
Add honesty boundaries to audit output ('you can fix X yourself, Y needs a dev, Z needs an agency')
